channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: How a cheese sandwich at the Masters is still $1.50 in an era of price hikes
item.description: The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.

item.title: 710,000 fewer babies were born last year in U.S. compared with two decades ago
item.description: The U.S. fertility rate continued its slide to historic levels, due to plunging teen pregnancies and far more women delaying motherhood into their 30s and 40s.

item.title: Israel’s Netanyahu calls for talks with Lebanon ‘as soon as possible’
item.description: Israeli PM says his gov't ready to enter negotiations on 'disarming Hezbollah' and 'establishing peaceful relations'.

item.title: Zohran Mamdani on his first 100 days
item.description: 100 days in, NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani faces a performance review. We asked New Yorkers about his record.

item.title: Zohran Mamdani on 100 days as New York mayor
item.description: NYC’s mayor reflects on childcare, housing, bigotry, Iran and the future of the Democratic Party.

item.title: UK says three Russian submarines tracked during ‘covert’ operation
item.description: British defence minister says the operation did not find evidence that Russia had damaged undersea infrastructure.

item.title: ‘Not some piece of ice’: Greenland hits back at Trump insult
item.description: PM Nielsen calls Greenland a proud nation seeking to maintain global order, after US president calls it 'piece of ice'.

item.title: Iranians rally nationwide to honour late leader Ali Khamenei
item.description: Thousands gathered across Iran to honor Ali Khamenei, whose assassination marked the start of the US-Israeli attack.

item.title: Do turmeric supplements really treat pain, boost mood, and improve allergies? Experts say they work best for 2 conditions
item.description: Experts weigh in on the science behind the hype over popular turmeric supplements.

item.title: Levi’s 517 jeans sales jump 25% thanks to ‘Love Story’ and the Carolyn Bessette Kennedy effect
item.description: Shares of Levi Strauss jumped back into positive territory for the year yesterday.

item.title: ‘I hate working 5 days’: Zoom CEO says traditional work schedules are becoming obsolete—and predicts a 3-day workweek by 2031
item.description: “I do not think we need to work for 5 days because literally we all will employ so many digital agents,” Zoom CEO Eric Yuan said.

item.title: Amid the ‘SaaSpocalypse,’ CIOs and CTOs take a harder line with their vendors
item.description: As investors debate the threat that AI poses to enterprise software companies, CIOs are on the font lines of the changes affecting the industry.

item.title: Brazil Weighs Raising Ethanol Blend in Boost to Consumers, Mills
item.description: The world’s second-largest ethanol producer Brazil is considering raising the amount of ethanol in gasoline, in a move that would aid consumers facing higher fuel prices while also boosting demand for sugar-cane mills struggling with a biofuel supply glut.

item.title: Banxico’s Dissenters Warn About Price Pressures Due to Iran War
item.description: Mexico’s central bank’s decision to cut interest rates last week faced strong opposition from two of its five board members, who raised concerns about lingering inflationary pressures from the war in Iran.

item.title: Mercuria Trader Who Built Huge LME Aluminum Bets Has Left
item.description: Sonny McNess, the veteran trader who helped build such massive aluminum positions at Mercuria Energy Group that the London Metal Exchange was forced to adjust its rules, has left the trading house.

item.title: Nigeria’s Dollar Reserves Dwindle as Central Bank Defends Naira
item.description: Nigeria‘s naira has stayed largely stable through the market volatility sparked by the Iran war, but that came at a cost to the nation’s foreign-exchange reserves.

item.title: Modelo Owner Constellation Gives Softer-Than-Expected Outlook
item.description: Constellation Brands Inc. projected slower-than-expected growth for its beer business this year, renewing concern that declines in alcohol consumption have yet to hit their floor. Organic beer sales, which measure changes in sales volumes, are seen in a range of down 1% to up 1% in the company’s current year, Constellation said Wednesday. The range trails analysts’ average estimate for a 2.2% increase. While the guidance represents an improvement from the previous fiscal year, which ended Feb. 28, the lower-than-expected outlook suggests even popular brands such as Modelo and Corona are struggling to sidestep a broader decline in the category. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Oil Trading Costs Surge as ICE Hikes Crude, Diesel Margins
item.description: Intercontinental Exchange Inc. will boost the margins traders have to post for its Brent crude and European diesel futures contracts as the war in Iran sends volatility surging across commodity markets.

item.title: DOJ Opens Probe Into NFL’s Sports Television Deals
item.description: The US Justice Department has opened an antitrust probe into the National Football League, looking at whether its broadcast deals with media companies drive up the costs for consumers, according to people familiar with the matter. The NFL and other sports leagues currently have an antitrust exemption that allows them to negotiate media deals collectively on behalf of their teams under the 1961 Sports Broadcasting Act. But media outlets, regulators and members of Congress have recently highlighted how difficult it is for consumers to be able to watch games amid a labyrinth of streaming, broadcast and cable options. Policy experts have begun to debate whether the act applies to negotiations beyond free broadcast TV distribution. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: NLB Joins Raiffeisen in Bidding Battle for Balkans Lender Addiko
item.description: Nova Ljubljanska banka d.d. said it plans to announce a purchase offer for shares in Balkans consumer lending specialist Addiko Bank AG, countering a similar proposal announced less than a day ago by Raiffeisen Bank International AG.

item.title: Carney’s Rival Says He Must Help Homeowners in Indigenous Case
item.description: The political debate over a court ruling recognizing Indigenous land rights in the Vancouver area escalated on Thursday after Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre urged the prime minister to intervene.

item.title: Israel Says Agrees to Direct Talks With Lebanon
item.description:
Natasha Hall, associate fellow at Chatham House, said the negotiations on the ceasefire in the Iran war are complicated by fundamental issues that have driven conflict in the region for years.
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says that he has agreed to direct talks with Lebanon. Stating that “In light of Lebanon’s repeated calls to open direct negotiations with Israel, I instructed the Cabinet yesterday to open direct negotiations with Lebanon as soon as possible” The negotiations will focus on disarming Hezbollah and establishing peaceful relations between Israel and Lebanon. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Venezuelan Cops Block March as Pay Pledge Fails to Calm Workers
item.description: Venezuelan security forces blocked an anti-government march from reaching the presidential palace on Thursday, after a vague pledge to raise wages failed to calm workers angered by soaring prices.

item.title: Blackstone Unit Lends $226 Million for US Outdoor Storage Lots
item.description: A unit of Blackstone Inc. provided a $226 million loan for a group of industrial outdoor storage facilities concentrated in the US Sunbelt.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic Still Blocked as Only Iran-Linked Ships Cross
item.description:
Ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained blocked Thursday, limited to a handful of Iran-linked ships, another sign that a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran has yet to improve flows through the world’s key energy chokepoint. Just seven vessels, all of which have either called at Iranian ports or were carrying Iranian cargo, were observed making the voyage out of the Persian Gulf on Wednesday and into Thursday morning. Normal transits in both directions are more like 135 a day.
Michelle Brouhard, Head of Policy and Geopolitical Risk at Kpler joins to discuss what this means for oil market moving forward. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Glencore and Mercuria Agree to Buy More LNG From Commonwealth
item.description: Glencore Plc. and Mercuria Energy Trading SA agreed to increase liquefied natural gas purchases under 20-year deals with an export terminal Kimmeridge Energy Management Co. is developing in Louisiana, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Oil Rises in Choppy Session as Saudi Supplies Hit By Attacks
item.description: Oil climbed in choppy trading on signs of further Middle Eastern supply losses and as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is still largely blocked.

item.title: Стармер: переконався, що перемир’я у конфлікті з Іраном є крихким
item.description: Прем’єр-міністр Великої Британії Кір Стармер розповів, що після переговорів з лідерами країн Перської затоки переконався, що перемир’я у конфлікті з Іраном є "крихким".

item.title: Італія погіршить прогноз зростання ВВП через високі ціни на енергоресурси
item.description: Італія готується погіршити прогноз зростання ВВП країни через негативний вплив війни на Близькому Сході.

item.title: Посол США в ЄС переконує, що Трамп і Венс не втручаються у вибори в Угорщині
item.description: Посол США в ЄС Ендрю Пуздер переконує, що президент Дональд Трамп та віцепрезидент Джей Ді Венс не втручаються у вибори в Угорщині попри їхню підтримку чинного прем’єр-міністра Віктора Орбана.

item.title: Каллас назвала несправедливою критику Європи через дії на Близькому Сході
item.description: Висока представниця ЄС із закордонних справ і політики безпеки Кая Каллас відкинула критику на адресу Європи, згідно з якою вона не робить достатньо на Близькому Сході.

item.title: Норвегія засудила удари Ізраїлю по Лівану, що призвели до масової загибелі цивільних
item.description: Норвегія засудила масовані удари Ізраїлю по території Лівану у межах протистояння з проіранською "Хезболлою", через які за короткий час загинули близько 200 людей і постраждала цивільна інфраструктура.

item.title: Рютте розповів, як Європа поспішно намагається допомогти з Іраном після нападок Трампа
item.description: Генеральний секретар НАТО Марк Рютте виступив на захист реакції союзників на війну з Іраном, водночас намагаючись запевнити світову спільноту в тому, що існування Альянсу не перебуває під загрозою.

item.title: У Туреччині влаштували масштабний рейд проти ІДІЛ після нападу біля консульства Ізраїлю
item.description: У Туреччині заявили про затримання близько 200 осіб по всій території країни у межах рейду проти ІДІЛ після нападу поблизу консульства Ізраїлю у Стамбулі.

item.title: Угорці не вірять Орбану, Молдова вийшла з СНД, Естонія готується до війни: новини дня
item.description: Очільник Генштабу Франції назвав війну з Росією пріоритетом оборони. Париж планує подвоїти покарання для суден "тіньового флоту".

item.title: ЗМІ: Трамп попросив прем’єра Ізраїлю послабити удари по Лівану
item.description: Президент США Дональд Трамп в останній розмові з прем’єр-міністром Ізраїлю Біньяміном Нетаньягу закликав його послабити удари по Лівану та наполягати на перемовинах.

item.title: ЗМІ: Трамп думає зменшити кількість військових США у Європі через невдоволення союзниками
item.description: Президент США Дональд Трамп нібито розглядає зменшення чисельності американських військ у Європі через своє невдоволення тим, як європейські союзники відмежувались від його війни проти Ірану та "не зрозуміли" бажання анексувати Гренландію.

item.title: Pink to host 2026 Tony awards: ‘It’s the honor of an entire lifetime’
item.description:
The artist will follow in the footsteps of Ariana DeBose, Cynthia Erivo and Kevin Spacey this June

The Tony awards have turned to a singer with a reputation for a high-energy, physical live show to be the next telecast host – Pink.

The three-time Grammy award winner will make her debut as emcee for the awards on 7 June at its familiar home of Radio City Music Hall.

Continue reading...

item.title: UK to give £380m grant to Tata battery factory in Somerset
item.description:
Investment in plant due to supply batteries to Jaguar Land Rover expected to boost economic growth and secure jobs

The Somerset battery factory due to supply Jaguar Land Rover is to receive £380m in UK government funding as it pushes ahead with construction despite delays.

JLR, Britain’s largest automotive employer, is due to receive batteries from the site to make electric versions of its Range Rover and Jaguar models. The Indian conglomerate Tata owns JLR and the electric vehicle (EV) battery factory under its Agratas subsidiary.

Continue reading...

item.title: Netanyahu calls for talks with Lebanon after bombardment of Beirut threatens ceasefire
item.description:
Israeli PM gives no indication bombing will stop after strikes that killed more than 200 people led to global outcry

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Benjamin Netanyahu has called for negotiations with Lebanon after worldwide condemnation of Israel’s intense bombardment of Beirut and other Lebanese cities, which threatened to undo the US-Iran ceasefire before it was barely a day old.

The Israeli prime minister said the talks should focus on the disarmament of Hezbollah and the establishment of “peace relations” with Lebanon, but gave no undertaking that the bombardment would stop, and there was no immediate sign of a let up in Israeli strikes. The Lebanese government had requested a ceasefire before talks began.

Continue reading...

item.title: Middle East crisis live: Israel ready ’to begin direct negotiations’ with Lebanon as Trump ‘optimistic’ about fragile ceasefire
item.description:
Lebanese president says ceasefire must be in place before any talks; US president reportedly claims Iran’s leaders ‘talk much differently’ in private meetings

  • Israel’s bombing of Lebanon after US-Iran ceasefire prompts condemnation

The UK foreign minister, Yvette Cooper, has said Lebanon must be included in any ceasefire agreement. In other remarks now being reported by Reuters, Cooper added that shipping through the strait of Hormuz must be toll-free.

Amid ceasefire talks, Tehran has proposed fees or tolls on vessels to safely pass through the strait. Donald Trump on Wednesday suggested the US and Iran could collect tolls in a joint venture, while the White House said the priority was reopening the strait without limitations.

And my principles and values made sure that our decisions were that we wouldn’t get involved in the action without a lawful basis, without a viable, thought-through plan.”

Continue reading...

item.title: US accused of pressuring Latin America to cut ties with Cuban doctors program
item.description:
Cuba accuses US of ‘extorting’ countries in pushing them to axe deals with Havana to send doctors on medical missions

Cuba’s foreign minister has accused the United States of “extorting” Latin American countries by putting pressure on them to cancel decades-old deals with Havana for the supply of doctors.

Bruno Rodríguez said the United States was trying to “strangle” the economy of the communist island, which earns billions from its foreign medical missions, after several countries stopped deploying Cuban doctors.

Continue reading...

item.title: Wild chimpanzees recorded waging ‘civil war’ with coordinated attacks between two groups
item.description:
New study describes what may be the first case of a unified community of chimps, in Uganda, turning on itself

On a June day in 2015, primatologist Aaron Sandel was quietly observing a small cluster of the Ngogo chimpanzee group in Uganda’s Kibale national park when he noticed something strange. As other members of the chimpanzees’ wider group moved closer through the forest, the chimpanzees in front of him began to display nervous behaviour. They grimaced and touched each other for reassurance, acting more like they were about to meet strangers than close companions.

In hindsight, Sandel said, that moment was the first sign of what would become a years-long bloody conflict between a once close-knit group of chimps.

Continue reading...
